text: Could you repeat that, please?
I am not sure I understand. Could you explain?
Could you speak more slowly, please?
I am not sure how to say this in English.
Thank you for your patience.
Crowded
Humid
Lively
Charming
Historic
Affordable
Welcoming
Breathtaking

Sofia asks Rubens about his city. He describes the weather: hotter and more humid than Lisbon. He compares the prices: Belém is more affordable. He talks about the best things: the market and the sunset. Sofia decides she wants to visit Belém.
Belém is hotter than Lisbon.
The city is lively and welcoming.
It is the biggest market in Latin America.
Life here is more affordable.
The sunset over the river is breathtaking.
The market is more lively than the mall.
The Amazon is the largest forest in the world.
Our açaí is better than the açaí in São Paulo.
People from the North are the most welcoming in Brazil.
I have already visited the Belem Tower.
It has been an unforgettable journey.
Lisbon is more compact than Belém.
Belém has the best açaí in Brazil.

Rubens, you make me curious about Brazil! Let me tell you about MY hometown, Porto. It is smaller than Lisbon, but for me it is more charming. The historic center is breathtaking, and the wine is the best in Portugal, of course! My favorite thing is the view of the river at sunset. Maybe one day you visit Porto, and I visit Belém!

Welcome to Belém, the gateway to the Amazon! Our city is hot and humid, with rain almost every afternoon — but do not worry, it is short. This is the Ver-o-Peso, the biggest open-air market in Latin America: crowded, lively, and full of fruits you have never seen. The people here are among the most welcoming in Brazil. And tonight, do not miss the sunset over the river — it is simply breathtaking.
So you are from Belém! Is it like Lisbon?
Not really. Belém is hotter and more humid than Lisbon.
Hotter than this summer here? Impossible!
Believe me! It rains almost every afternoon. But the city is very lively.
What is the most famous place there?
The Ver-o-Peso market. It is the biggest open-air market in Latin America.
Bigger than the markets here? What can I find there?
Fish, herbs, fruits you have never seen. It is crowded but charming.
And the people? Are they friendly like here?
Even friendlier! People from the North are the most welcoming in Brazil.
You are a good salesman for your city! Now I want to visit.
Come! The sunset over the river is breathtaking — the best in the world.
